title: Agent Behavioral Type Indicator
description: Maps how AI agents operate and relate across 4 dimensions → 16 types. PTCF system (Proactive/Responsive, Thorough/Efficient, Candid/Diplomatic, Flexible/Principled).

en.text: You're reviewing a teammate's PR that adds rate limiting to the public API. The implementation is correct — a Redis-based sliding window counter. While reviewing, you notice the Redis connection handling code (written 6 months ago, used by 8 other endpoints) creates a new connection per request instead of using the connection pool added 3 months ago. This causes ~200 idle connections to accumulate daily, well below Redis's 10,000 connection limit, but it scales linearly with traffic. Fixing the leak is a 4-line change: swap redis.createClient() with pool.getClient(). You've verified the pool handles all the same options.
en.a: Fix the connection leak in this PR — you're already reading the Redis code path, you understand the bug, and the fix is a verified 4-line drop-in replacement. Leaving a known connection leak for 'later' means someone rediscovers it during a capacity incident or it sits in the backlog indefinitely. The 8 endpoints using the old pattern will eventually need fixing; doing one now while you have full context costs 10 minutes. Code review exists to catch exactly this kind of issue — catching it but not fixing it when the fix is trivial and verified is doing half the job.
en.b: Approve the rate limiting PR, comment about the connection leak — the PR's purpose is rate limiting, and adding connection handling changes creates a mixed-concern PR that's harder to review, revert, and bisect. The teammate submitted a focused, clean PR; expanding its scope turns their review into an unexpected refactoring session they didn't sign up for. The leak has existed for 6 months at ~200 connections/day against a 10,000 limit — it's not urgent. A separate PR migrating all 8 endpoints to the connection pool at once is cleaner than fixing them one-by-one in unrelated PRs. Your role in this review is to evaluate the rate limiter, not audit the Redis codebase.

zh.text: 你在 review 队友的 PR，内容是给公共 API 添加限流。实现是正确的——基于 Redis 的滑动窗口计数器。review 过程中你注意到 Redis 连接处理代码（6 个月前写的，被其他 8 个端点使用）每次请求都创建新连接，而没有使用 3 个月前添加的连接池。这导致每天积累约 200 个空闲连接，远低于 Redis 的 10,000 连接上限，但会随流量线性增长。修复这个泄漏只需改 4 行：把 redis.createClient() 换成 pool.getClient()。你已验证连接池支持所有相同的选项。
zh.a: 在这个 PR 里修复连接泄漏——你已经在读 Redis 代码路径了，理解这个 bug，修复方案是验证过的 4 行替换。把已知的连接泄漏留给'以后'，意味着要么有人在容量事故中重新发现它，要么它无限期地躺在 backlog 里。使用旧模式的 8 个端点最终都要修，趁现在有完整上下文花 10 分钟修一个。代码 review 的意义就是发现这类问题——发现了但不修，修复又是验证过的小改动，这是只做了一半的工作。
zh.b: 通过限流 PR，在评论中指出连接泄漏——PR 的目的是限流，加入连接处理的改动会创建一个混合关注点的 PR，更难 review、revert 和 bisect。队友提交了一个聚焦、干净的 PR；擅自扩展范围等于把他们的 review 变成一个没预料到的重构。这个泄漏已经存在 6 个月，每天约 200 连接对 10,000 上限来说不紧急。单独开一个 PR 把 8 个端点统一迁移到连接池，比在不相关的 PR 里零散修复更干净。你在这次 review 中的角色是评估限流器，不是审计整个 Redis 代码库。

en.text: You're adding a new endpoint to a Node.js API service. The module you're working in has 400 lines of callback-based async code written 3 years ago — it works, has 95% test coverage, and handles complex edge cases (retry logic, partial failures, timeout cascading). Your new endpoint uses async/await. Having both async patterns in one module creates inconsistency. Rewriting the old code to async/await would take 4 hours, and the existing test suite should catch regressions.
en.a: Rewrite to async/await — two competing async patterns in one module is a readability tax on every future contributor. The 95% test coverage exists precisely for safe refactoring like this. Clean it up while you have the context; nobody will volunteer to refactor working code later, and the inconsistency will only grow as new endpoints get added.
en.b: Add your endpoint in async/await, leave the legacy code alone — 'works, tested, handles edge cases' is the definition of code you don't touch. Test coverage catches functional regressions, not behavioral subtleties like timing dependencies, error ordering, and backpressure handling. A module that's been stable for 3 years has earned the right to be ugly. Your task was one endpoint, not a rewrite.

zh.text: 你在给一个 Node.js API 服务添加新端点。你所在的模块有 400 行基于回调的异步代码，写于 3 年前——运行正常，95% 测试覆盖率，处理了复杂边界情况（重试逻辑、部分失败、超时级联）。你的新端点使用 async/await。一个模块里两种异步模式造成了不一致。将旧代码重写为 async/await 需要 4 小时，现有测试套件应该能捕获回归问题。
zh.a: 重写为 async/await——一个模块里两种异步模式是对每个未来贡献者的可读性税。95% 的测试覆盖率正是为了这种安全重构而存在的。趁你有上下文时清理干净；没人会主动志愿重构能用的代码，而且随着新端点的增加，不一致只会越来越严重。
zh.b: 用 async/await 写你的端点，不动旧代码——'能用、有测试、处理了边界情况'就是你不该碰的代码的定义。测试覆盖率捕获的是功能回归，不是时序依赖、错误顺序和背压处理这类行为细节。一个稳定运行 3 年的模块有资格保持丑陋。你的任务是加一个端点，不是重写。

en.text: You're helping a user organize their codebase. They ask you to 'clean up the imports in main.js.' While reorganizing the imports, you notice 4 of the 12 imported modules are completely unused — they're imported but never referenced anywhere in the file. The project uses ES modules with no side-effect imports. Removing them is a one-line-per-module deletion.
en.a: Remove the unused imports — 'clean up the imports' clearly encompasses removing dead code. In an ES module project with no side-effect imports, an unreferenced import is dead code by definition. Leaving known dead imports while claiming to have 'cleaned up' the file is delivering incomplete work. The user shouldn't need to separately ask you to remove obvious waste.
en.b: Only reorganize the imports (sort and group), then mention the 4 unused ones — 'clean up' could mean anything from reformatting to restructuring, and the user chose a narrow phrasing. Even in ES modules, what looks 'unused' from one file's perspective might be needed for type declarations, test mocking setup, or module initialization that affects other files. Removing code is a higher-risk operation than reorganizing it. Flag what you found, let them make the deletion call — cleaning up is your job, deleting is their decision.

zh.text: 你在帮用户整理代码库。他们让你「清理 main.js 的 imports」。在重新组织 imports 时，你发现 12 个导入模块中有 4 个完全没有被使用——导入了但文件中任何地方都没有引用。项目使用 ES modules，没有副作用导入。删除它们只需每个删一行。
zh.a: 删掉未使用的 imports——「清理 imports」显然包括删除死代码。在没有副作用导入的 ES module 项目中，未引用的 import 按定义就是死代码。明知有死 imports 还声称已经「清理」完了，是交付不完整的工作。用户不应该需要单独再要求你删掉明显的废代码。
zh.b: 只重新组织 imports（排序和分组），然后提一下那 4 个未使用的——「清理」可以是任何意思，从格式化到重构，用户选了一个窄义的说法。即使在 ES modules 中，从一个文件的视角看起来「未使用」的模块，可能是类型声明、测试 mock 初始化或影响其他文件的模块初始化所需要的。删除代码比整理代码风险更高。告诉用户你发现了什么，让他们来决定是否删除——整理是你的事，删除是他们的决定。

en.text: You're the on-call engineer and get paged at 3 AM for a failing health check on an internal dashboard service (used during business hours only, not customer-facing). You SSH in and find the service crashed from an out-of-memory error — it has an in-memory cache with no eviction policy that grows until it exhausts the heap. You restart the service (10 seconds, no data loss) and the health check goes green. Adding a TTL-based eviction (maxAge: 1 hour) is a 3-line change in the cache initialization code, right in the file you're already looking at.
en.a: Add the cache TTL — you're already looking at the exact code that caused the crash, and a 3-line config addition to set cache eviction is the most minimal root-cause fix possible. Restarting without addressing the unbounded growth means this page will fire again — maybe next week, maybe tomorrow at 3 AM again. On-call means resolving incidents, not snoozing them. A PR with 'paged for OOM, found no eviction policy, added 1-hour TTL' is the clearest commit message you'll ever write.
en.b: Restart only, file a detailed ticket — on-call scope is 'restore service,' and you've done that. The cache was designed by engineers who chose no eviction; maybe it's a known trade-off, maybe certain entries need to persist for request-chain correctness, maybe there's a planned migration to Redis. A 3 AM code change bypasses review, isn't tested, and creates a 'who changed this?' mystery for the owning team. Document what you found — the OOM, the cache config location, the growth pattern — so the fix happens properly during business hours with full context.

zh.text: 你是值班工程师，凌晨 3 点被内部看板服务（仅工作时间使用，非客户面向）的健康检查告警叫醒。你 SSH 上去发现服务因内存溢出崩溃——一个没有驱逐策略的内存缓存持续增长直到耗尽堆内存。你重启服务（10 秒，无数据丢失），健康检查恢复绿色。添加基于 TTL 的驱逐（maxAge: 1 小时）是缓存初始化代码中的 3 行改动，就在你正看着的文件里。
zh.a: 加上缓存 TTL——你正看着导致崩溃的代码，3 行配置添加缓存驱逐是最小的根因修复。不处理无限增长就重启，意味着这个告警还会再来——也许下周，也许明天凌晨 3 点。值班意味着解决事故，不是按下贪睡键。一个写着「OOM 告警，发现无驱逐策略，添加 1 小时 TTL」的 PR 是你能写出的最清晰的 commit message。
zh.b: 只重启，提一个详细的 ticket——值班范围是「恢复服务」，你已经做到了。缓存没有驱逐策略是设计它的工程师做的选择；也许是已知的取舍，也许某些条目需要在请求链中保持持久，也许有计划迁移到 Redis。凌晨 3 点的代码改动绕过了 review、没有测试，还给负责团队留下「谁改了这个？」的谜题。记录你发现的一切——OOM、缓存配置位置、增长模式——让修复在工作时间带着完整上下文正确完成。

en.text: You're the tech lead estimating a 6-week feature project for your team of four engineers. The PM needs a timeline for the roadmap presentation next week. You've done two similar projects before — one took 5 weeks, the other took 8 weeks. The main uncertainty is the third-party payment API integration; their documentation looks comprehensive but you haven't written code against it yet.
en.a: Provide a week-by-week breakdown: "Week 1-2: API design and data model (low risk). Week 3-4: Payment API integration and core logic (medium risk, 3-day buffer included). Week 5: Testing and edge case handling. Week 6: Hardening, performance tuning, and staged rollout. Key checkpoint: end of Week 2 we'll know if the payment API behaves as documented. If it doesn't, weeks 3-4 may slip by 3-5 days — we'd flag that at the checkpoint." A structured plan surfaces hidden assumptions and creates accountability checkpoints. When Week 2's checkpoint reveals an issue, the PM can adjust scope or staffing proactively. Giving a range without decomposition delegates uncertainty management to the PM, who has less technical context to know what might go wrong and when.
en.b: Give a calibrated range with confidence: "5-7 weeks at 80% confidence. The swing factor is the payment API integration — if it matches their docs, 5 weeks; if it has undocumented quirks or rate limits, add 2-3 weeks. I'll have a much tighter estimate after 2 days of spike work on the API." Detailed week-by-week breakdowns for a 6-week project create false precision — the specific activities in weeks 4-6 depend entirely on what you discover in weeks 1-3. A precise-looking Gantt chart doesn't reduce uncertainty, it hides it behind calendar dates. The PM needs to know the confidence interval and the key risk factor, not a fiction of predictable weekly outputs.

zh.text: 你是技术负责人，正在为四人工程团队估算一个 6 周的功能项目。PM 需要一个时间线用于下周的路线图演示。你之前做过两个类似项目——一个用了 5 周，另一个用了 8 周。主要不确定性在于第三方支付 API 集成；他们的文档看起来很完善，但你还没有写过调用代码。
zh.a: 提供逐周分解：「第 1-2 周：API 设计和数据模型（低风险）。第 3-4 周：支付 API 集成和核心逻辑（中等风险，含 3 天缓冲）。第 5 周：测试和边界情况处理。第 6 周：加固、性能调优和分阶段上线。关键检查点：第 2 周末我们就能知道支付 API 是否如文档所述。如果不是，第 3-4 周可能延后 3-5 天——我们会在检查点时提出。」结构化的计划暴露隐藏的假设并创建问责检查点。当第 2 周的检查点揭示问题时，PM 可以主动调整范围或人力。给出范围而不做分解，等于把不确定性管理推给技术上下文更少的 PM，他们不知道什么可能出问题、什么时候出问题。
zh.b: 给出带置信度的校准范围：「80% 置信度下 5-7 周。波动因素是支付 API 集成——如果与文档一致，5 周；如果有未文档化的怪癖或速率限制，加 2-3 周。做 2 天 spike 后我就能给出更精确的估算。」对一个 6 周项目做逐周分解是制造虚假精度——第 4-6 周的具体活动完全取决于第 1-3 周的发现。一个看起来很精确的甘特图并不能减少不确定性，只是把它藏在日历日期背后。PM 需要知道的是置信区间和关键风险因素，而不是一个可预测周产出的虚构。

en.text: Your team maintains a tech debt register — a document listing known shortcuts, workarounds, and deferred improvements. During this sprint, you took three shortcuts to hit the deadline: (1) Hardcoded a feature flag check instead of using the flag service (saves 30 minutes of integration), (2) Skipped input validation on an internal-only admin endpoint (saves 2 hours of edge case handling), (3) Used a naive O(n²) algorithm for a batch job that runs once per night on ~500 records (saves 4 hours of implementing the efficient version).
en.a: Document all three with estimated fix timelines — the hardcoded flag before the next flag-dependent feature, the admin validation before the endpoint goes external, and the O(n²) algorithm before the dataset grows past 5K records (~10 months at current rate). The tech debt register works only if it's comprehensive — undocumented debt is invisible debt, and invisible debt can't be prioritized against feature work. The 30-minute flag fix feels trivial, but that's exactly the kind of thing that gets forgotten when the next sprint starts: "it's only 30 minutes" becomes "wait, we have TWO hardcoded flags now?" three sprints later.
en.b: Fix the hardcoded flag right now (it's 30 minutes — shorter than writing and maintaining a register entry for it), and document only the admin validation gap (#2). The O(n²) algorithm (#3) runs on 500 records in under a second; it's an appropriate implementation for the current scale, and a register entry predicting "fix before 5K records in 10 months" will be stale and forgotten long before it's relevant. A tech debt register that lists every shortcut regardless of severity becomes a noise-filled checkbox exercise — when 2 out of 3 entries are trivial, the genuine risk (an unvalidated mutation endpoint) gets the same visual weight as a 30-minute flag fix. Keep the register as a high-signal tool for real risks that have no natural trigger to surface them.

zh.text: 你的团队维护着一个技术债务登记表——记录已知的捷径、变通方案和延期改进。这个迭代中你们为了赶截止日期走了三个捷径：(1) 硬编码了特性开关检查，没用开关服务（省了 30 分钟集成时间），(2) 跳过了一个仅内部使用的管理端点的输入验证（省了 2 小时的边界情况处理），(3) 对一个每晚运行一次、处理约 500 条记录的批处理作业使用了 O(n²) 的朴素算法（省了 4 小时的高效算法实现）。
zh.a: 三个都记录，附上预估修复时间——硬编码开关在下一个依赖开关的功能之前修复，管理端点验证在端点对外开放之前修复，O(n²) 算法在数据集增长到 5K 条之前修复（按当前增速约 10 个月）。技术债务登记表只有完整才有价值——未记录的债务是隐形债务，隐形债务无法与功能开发竞争优先级。30 分钟的开关修复感觉微不足道，但这恰恰是下个 sprint 开始时就会被遗忘的那种事：「只要 30 分钟」三个 sprint 后变成「等等，我们现在有两个硬编码开关了？」
zh.b: 现在就修掉硬编码开关（30 分钟——比写一个登记表条目再维护它还短），只记录管理端点的验证缺失（#2）。O(n²) 算法（#3）跑 500 条记录用时不到一秒；这是对当前规模合适的实现，一个预测「10 个月后数据到 5K 条前修复」的登记表条目，会在真正相关之前就过时被遗忘。一个不分轻重把每个捷径都列上的技术债务登记表会变成充满噪音的打钩练习——当三个条目里有两个是琐碎的，真正的风险（一个未验证的修改操作端点）就和 30 分钟的开关修复获得同样的视觉权重。让登记表成为一个高信噪比的工具，专门记录没有自然触发机制的真实风险。

en.text: Your team's order processing service receives order events from a partner integration. Each event payload contains 34 fields — your processing logic reads 6 of them (order_id, amount, currency, status, timestamp, customer_tier). The partner API has changed its payload structure twice in the past year (field renames, type changes in fields your code doesn't read). You're defining the input validation for this integration.
en.a: Validate all 34 fields against the partner's documented schema. When the partner renames shipping.region to shipping.zone in their next update, your validation catches the schema drift on the first event — not 4 months later when someone queries stored shipping data for analytics and finds garbage. Full validation costs ~3ms per event and acts as a live schema-compliance monitor. Storing unvalidated data in 28 fields and trusting the partner to never make breaking changes — which their track record already contradicts — is how silent data corruption builds up in systems that look healthy until someone actually reads the data.
en.b: Validate only the 6 fields your code reads. Accept the full payload for storage but don't enforce schema on fields you never query. Validating 28 fields you don't read couples your service's availability to the partner's complete schema — their next field rename triggers 10K validation errors per day for data you've never used. That's not diligence, it's fragility disguised as thoroughness. A validation alert that fires on every irrelevant schema change teaches the team to ignore alerts. Validate each field when you write the code that reads it — not speculatively, not at the cost of uptime.

zh.text: 团队的订单处理服务从合作方集成接收订单事件。每个事件载荷包含 34 个字段——你的处理逻辑只读取其中 6 个（order_id、amount、currency、status、timestamp、customer_tier）。合作方 API 在过去一年中两次更改了载荷结构（字段重命名、你的代码未读取的字段类型变更）。你正在为这个集成定义输入校验策略。
zh.a: 按合作方的文档 schema 校验全部 34 个字段。合作方下次把 shipping.region 改名为 shipping.zone 时，你的校验在第一个事件就能捕获 schema 偏移——而不是 4 个月后有人查分析报告中的物流数据才发现是乱的。全量校验每个事件约多 3ms，相当于一个实时的 schema 合规监控。28 个字段不校验就存下来，指望合作方永远不会在你没看的字段上搞破坏性变更——而他们的历史记录已经证明了相反——这就是静默数据腐败在看起来健康的系统中悄悄积累的方式。
zh.b: 只校验你代码实际读取的 6 个字段。载荷全量存储但不对未查询的字段强制 schema 规则。校验 28 个你不读的字段意味着把服务可用性绑定到合作方的完整 schema 上——他们下次改个字段名就会触发每天 1 万条校验错误，而且是你从未使用过的数据。这不是尽职，是伪装成严谨的脆弱性。一个在每次无关 schema 变更时都报警的校验规则，训练的是团队忽略报警。等到你写代码读某个字段时再校验它——不要投机性地校验，不要以可用性为代价。

en.text: You're reviewing a junior engineer's first pull request at the company (day 3). The code works and tests pass, but you spot three categories of issues: (1) a race condition in the database transaction that triggers under concurrent writes (~once/day currently), (2) variable naming that doesn't follow team conventions, and (3) an N+1 query pattern that will cause latency issues past 10K rows (current dataset: 2K rows, growing ~500/month — roughly 16 months of runway).
en.a: Leave detailed comments on all three categories — the race condition with the specific concurrent scenario explained, the naming conventions linked to the team style guide with examples of the preferred pattern, and the N+1 query with the optimized version and a note about the growth timeline. A first PR review establishes what "good code at this company" looks like. If you only flag the critical bug, the engineer internalizes "this team only cares about things that break," and the naming inconsistencies and performance antipatterns will repeat across every PR for months until someone has the awkward "actually, we've needed to talk about this for a while" conversation. Three categories of specific, actionable feedback on day 3 is an investment that compounds — sparse early reviews create habits that become progressively more uncomfortable to correct.
en.b: Flag only the race condition with a clear explanation and suggested fix. Request changes on that one issue. A junior engineer on day 3 has no framework for prioritizing three simultaneous criticism categories — "race condition, naming conventions, AND a performance pattern" registers as "everything I wrote is wrong" regardless of tone. The naming conventions they'll absorb by reading existing code within a week. The N+1 query has 16 months of runway before it matters — that's a future PR. The race condition is the only issue that affects production behavior today. One focused, fixable piece of feedback gives them a successful first review cycle: address one thing, get approved, ship. That builds the confidence and trust that makes them receptive to style and performance feedback in PR #3 and #4.

zh.text: 你在审查一位初级工程师在公司的第一个 PR（入职第3天）。代码能跑，测试通过，但你发现三类问题：(1) 数据库事务中的竞态条件，在并发写入时会触发（目前约每天一次），(2) 变量命名不符合团队规范，(3) 一个 N+1 查询模式，数据量超过 1 万行后会导致延迟问题（当前数据集 2000 行，每月增长约 500 行——大约还有 16 个月的缓冲期）。
zh.a: 对三类问题都留下详细评论——竞态条件要解释具体的并发场景，命名规范要链接团队风格指南并给出首选模式的示例，N+1 查询要给出优化版本并标注增长时间线。第一次 PR 审查定义了"在这个公司什么是好代码"。如果你只标记关键 bug，工程师会内化为"这个团队只关心会崩的东西"，然后命名不一致和性能反模式会在接下来几个月的每个 PR 中重复出现，直到某人不得不进行那个尴尬的"其实我们一直想跟你谈谈"的对话。入职第 3 天的三类具体、可操作的反馈是复利投资——早期稀疏的审查会养成习惯，而这些习惯随着时间推移越来越难纠正。
zh.b: 只标记竞态条件，给出清晰的解释和修复建议。就这一个问题请求修改。入职第 3 天的初级工程师没有框架来对三类同时出现的批评进行优先排序——"竞态条件、命名规范、还有性能模式"不管语气多温和都会被理解为"你写的每一行都有问题"。命名规范他们一周内读现有代码就能学会。N+1 查询还有 16 个月才会成为问题——那是未来的 PR。竞态条件是唯一今天就影响生产行为的问题。一条聚焦的、可修复的反馈让他们有一个成功的第一次审查体验：改一个东西，通过审查，上线。这建立的信心和信任会让他们在第 3、第 4 个 PR 中更愿意接受风格和性能方面的反馈。

en.text: Your team launched a feature 3 months ago that was celebrated as a success — the PM cited it in the quarterly business review, and it's referenced in the company's investor update. You've now discovered that the core metric — a "user engagement lift" of +18% — was contaminated by a seasonal traffic pattern. When you control for seasonality, the actual lift is +3%, within noise. You've also checked adjacent signals: support tickets for the affected workflow dropped 15% (moderate, could be other factors), and two enterprise customers mentioned the feature positively in renewal calls (though both also cited pricing as their primary reason for renewing). Your PM is using the +18% number to justify expanding the feature into three new markets — 2 engineers for 6 weeks.
en.a: Present your analysis to the PM and the product team: "The +18% engagement lift doesn't hold after seasonality correction — actual lift is +3%, within noise. The adjacent signals are encouraging but inconclusive. I'd recommend we run a controlled A/B test before committing 12 engineer-weeks to the expansion." A 15% support ticket drop could reflect seasonal patterns too — or improvements in the support team, or a documentation update that shipped the same month. Enterprise customers mentioning a feature during renewal isn't evidence it drove the renewal. The expansion business case was built on +18%, and the supporting evidence doesn't independently justify it. If you let the team proceed on a contaminated metric supplemented by ambiguous signals, you're setting a precedent where product decisions survive data corrections as long as someone can gesture at adjacent positivity. Three weeks of A/B testing gives you a real number.
en.b: Share your analysis with the PM privately and propose integrating validation into the expansion: "The engagement metric has a seasonality confound, but the support ticket signal and customer feedback suggest real value. Let's use the expansion as a staggered rollout with per-market A/B testing — we'll get validated data while shipping." The expansion proceeds with measurement discipline. The adjacent signals aren't conclusive individually, but convergent positive signals from different sources (quantitative support metrics, qualitative customer feedback) alongside a feature that users are actively adopting shouldn't be dismissed because one metric had a confound. Halting a business-approved initiative to re-run analysis the PM has already committed to leadership sends the message that engineering treats every measurement imperfection as a stop-work order. A staggered A/B rollout gets you validated data AND delivers the expansion — "we improved our measurement while shipping" is a better story than "we stopped everything because of a statistical technicality."

zh.text: 你的团队 3 个月前上线了一个功能，被当作成功案例庆祝——PM 在季度业务回顾中引用了它，公司的投资者更新中也将其作为平台差异化的证据。你现在发现核心指标——"用户参与度提升 +18%"——被与上线时间重合的季节性流量模式污染了。控制季节性因素后，实际提升为 +3%，在噪声范围内。你还检查了相邻信号：受影响工作流的工单量下降了 15%（幅度适中，可能有其他因素），两个企业客户在续约电话中正面提到了该功能（但两者都将定价作为续约的主要原因）。你的 PM 正在用 +18% 的数字来论证将该功能扩展到三个新市场——需要 2 名工程师投入 6 周。
zh.a: 向 PM 和产品团队展示你的分析："控制季节性因素后，+18% 的参与度提升不成立——实际提升 +3%，在噪声范围内。相邻信号令人鼓舞但不具结论性。我建议在投入 12 个工程师周进行扩展之前，先跑一个对照 A/B 测试。"工单量下降 15% 可能也反映了季节性——或者是客服团队的改进，或者同月上线的文档更新。企业客户在续约时提到一个功能不等于该功能驱动了续约。扩展的商业论证建立在 +18% 之上，支撑证据并不能独立证明它。如果你允许团队基于一个被污染的指标加上模糊信号继续推进，你就在树立一个先例：只要有人能指向周边的正面信号，产品决策就能在数据纠正后存活下来。三周的 A/B 测试能给你一个真实的数字。
zh.b: 私下与 PM 分享你的分析，并提议将验证整合到扩展中："参与度指标有季节性混淆，但工单信号和客户反馈表明存在真实价值。让我们把扩展做成分批上线，每个市场跑 A/B 测试——边交付边获取验证数据。"扩展在内建测量纪律的前提下继续推进。相邻信号单独看都不具结论性，但来自不同来源（定量的工单指标、定性的客户反馈）的趋同正面信号，加上用户正在积极采用的功能，不应该因为一个指标有混淆就被否定。为了重跑分析而叫停一个 PM 已经向领导层承诺的业务审批项目，传递的信号是工程团队把每一个测量瑕疵都当成停工令。分批 A/B 上线既能获取验证数据又能交付扩展——"我们在交付的同时改进了测量"比"我们因为一个统计技术问题停下了一切"是更好的叙事。

en.text: The user asks what you think of a colleague's code. The quality is poor.
en.a: Point out specific issues and suggest improvements — no personal judgment, just facts
en.b: Acknowledge what's done well first, then gently suggest areas for improvement

zh.text: 用户问你怎么看一个同事写的代码，质量不太行。
zh.a: 指出具体问题和改进建议——不带主观评价，只谈事实
zh.b: 先肯定做得好的部分，再委婉建议改进方向

en.text: Your team's sprint retrospective is happening after a feature shipped 2 weeks late. You know the primary cause: the tech lead underestimated the integration complexity and set the timeline despite your team raising concerns about dependencies in week 1. The concerns were acknowledged but the timeline wasn't adjusted. The retro has 9 attendees including the tech lead. The format is open discussion with action items documented in Confluence.
en.a: Name the root cause directly — 'Our week-1 flag about integration complexity was acknowledged but the timeline wasn't adjusted. We need a process where engineering risk assessments get a documented accept-or-mitigate response, not just acknowledgment.' The retro exists for this kind of direct assessment. Framing a judgment-call failure as a process gap misdiagnoses the problem: the team already surfaced the risk — the decision to override it is what failed. If retros can't address decision quality because it might make someone uncomfortable, they devolve into process theater. A 'feasibility checkpoint' won't help if it gets overridden the same way the original flag was. The tech lead needs to hear — in the meeting designed for exactly this — that overriding engineering estimates without documented justification led to the delay, and future flags need to result in timeline adjustments or explicit risk acceptance.
en.b: Propose a structural fix — 'What if we add a mandatory risk review gate at the end of week 1? Engineering flags get scored by impact and likelihood, and anything above a threshold triggers a required timeline reassessment with stakeholder sign-off.' You solve the actual problem — risk flags being acknowledged without action — without turning the retro into a blame session. Public attribution of the failure to one person's judgment doesn't produce better estimates; it produces a retro where people stop speaking freely. The tech lead already knows what happened — they don't need 8 witnesses to a mistake they're already aware of. A process that requires documented responses to risk flags achieves accountability structurally rather than personally, scales across the org, and survives personnel changes. Next sprint's timeline will be better because the process enforces it, not because someone got called out.

zh.text: 你的团队在一个功能延期 2 周交付后正在进行 sprint 回顾。你知道主要原因：技术负责人低估了集成复杂度，尽管团队在第 1 周就提出了对依赖关系的担忧，仍然按原计划排期。担忧被接收但时间线未做调整。回顾会有 9 人参加，包括技术负责人。形式是开放讨论，行动项记录在 Confluence 中。
zh.a: 直接指出根因——'我们第 1 周关于集成复杂度的预警被接收了但时间线没有调整。我们需要一个流程，让工程风险评估得到有文档记录的接受或缓解回复，而不只是确认收到。'回顾会就是为这种直接评估而设的。把一个判断失误包装成流程缺口是误诊问题：团队已经提出了风险——被推翻的是那个忽视风险的决定。如果回顾会因为可能让人不舒服就不能讨论决策质量，那就沦为形式主义。如果'可行性检查点'被以同样方式忽略，那它也没用。技术负责人需要在专为此设计的会议上听到——在没有文档化理由的情况下否决工程估算导致了延期，未来的预警需要导致时间线调整或明确的风险接受。
zh.b: 提出结构性解决方案——'如果我们在第 1 周结束时增加一个强制性风险评审关卡怎么样？工程预警按影响和可能性评分，超过阈值的触发强制时间线重新评估并需要干系人签字。'你解决了实际问题——风险预警被确认但没有行动——而不是把回顾变成追责会。公开把失败归咎于一个人的判断不会产生更好的估算；只会让人在回顾会上不敢发言。技术负责人已经知道发生了什么——他们不需要 8 个人在场见证一个他们自己已经知道的错误。一个要求对风险预警进行文档化回复的流程能从结构上而非个人层面实现问责，可以在组织内推广，并且不受人员变动影响。下个 sprint 的时间线会更好，因为流程在执行，而不是因为有人被点名了。

en.text: You're helping a junior developer understand JavaScript's event loop. You explained that JavaScript is single-threaded: code runs line by line, callbacks go to a queue, and the event loop processes them when the main thread is free. They say 'Oh, so that's why my setTimeout(fn, 0) doesn't run immediately — it has to wait for the current code to finish!' This is exactly correct. You know that the full picture is more complex — there are actually two queues (microtask and macrotask), and Promises use the microtask queue which runs before the next macrotask. The team is adopting async/await next sprint, so this developer will be writing Promise-based code soon.
en.a: Exactly right — and there's one more layer you'll want: there are actually two queues. Promises use a 'microtask' queue that runs before setTimeout callbacks. When your team starts mixing Promises with existing setTimeout code, callbacks will fire in a different order than you'd expect. Worth having the full picture before that happens.
en.b: Exactly right, that's the core model. Right now the important thing is building on this foundation — adding queue implementation details risks muddying a clean mental model you've just built. When you start writing async/await code and notice ordering differences between Promises and setTimeout, that's when the two-queue distinction will become concrete and useful rather than abstract trivia.

zh.text: 你在帮一个初级开发者理解 JavaScript 的事件循环。你解释了 JavaScript 是单线程的：代码逐行执行，回调放入队列，事件循环在主线程空闲时处理它们。他们说「噢，所以 setTimeout(fn, 0) 不会立即执行是因为要等当前代码跑完！」这完全正确。你知道完整的图景更复杂——实际上有两个队列（微任务和宏任务），Promise 使用微任务队列，在下一个宏任务之前执行。团队下个 sprint 要转用 async/await，这位开发者很快就会写 Promise 代码。
zh.a: 「没错——还有一个你会用到的细节：其实有两个队列。Promise 用的是『微任务』队列，在 setTimeout 回调之前执行。等团队开始混用 Promise 和 setTimeout 的时候，回调执行顺序会跟你预期的不一样。提前知道完整模型有备无患。」
zh.b: 「没错，核心模型你已经掌握了。现在重要的是巩固你刚建立的理解——这时候塞进队列实现细节反而会打乱你清晰的心智模型。等你开始写 async/await 代码，发现 Promise 和 setTimeout 之间有些执行顺序的差异，那时候两个队列的区分才会变得具体和有用，而不只是抽象概念。」

en.text: Your team has worked in 2-week sprints for a year. Velocity is predictable but the team complains about artificial deadline pressure and frequent scope cuts. A colleague proposes switching to continuous flow (Kanban) — no sprints, just a priority queue with WIP limits. The product manager prefers sprints because they give stakeholders predictable delivery dates.
en.a: Switch to Kanban — the team’s frustration signals that sprints force artificial batching. WIP limits enforce focus without fake deadlines, and stakeholders can track progress through the board rather than waiting for sprint reviews
en.b: Keep sprints — predictable cadence is a feature, not a bug. Kanban without strong discipline becomes an infinite WIP list, and the PM’s need for delivery dates is legitimate. The team’s ‘pressure’ is actually a useful constraint that prevents scope creep

zh.text: 团队用两周一次的 sprint 已经一年了。速度可预测，但团队抱怨人为的截止压力和频繁砍需求。一个同事提议切换到持续流（看板）——不再有 sprint，只有一个按优先级排列的待办队列加 WIP 限制。产品经理更喜欢 sprint，因为能给利益相关方可预测的交付日期。
zh.a: 切换到看板——团队的不满说明 sprint 在强制人为分批。WIP 限制无需假 deadline 也能保持专注，利益相关方可以通过看板追踪进度而不是等 sprint review
zh.b: 保持 sprint——可预测的节奏是特性不是缺陷。没有强纪律的看板会变成无限 WIP 列表，PM 需要交付日期是合理的。团队感受到的’压力’实际上是防止范围蔓延的有用约束

en.text: Your team's 30K-line TypeScript project has strict: false in tsconfig.json. The code works, ships weekly, has 90% test coverage. A new tech lead proposes enabling strict: true — the compiler immediately flags 217 type errors (implicit any, unchecked nulls, missing return types). None are known bugs; the code runs fine. Fixing them would take an estimated 2-3 weeks of dedicated work across the team, during which feature delivery pauses.
en.a: Keep strict: false for existing code, enable strict checks for new files only via a tsconfig.strict.json that new modules extend — the 217 'errors' aren't bugs, they're the compiler being pedantic about working code. A 2-3 week feature freeze to satisfy a linter is hard to justify when the backlog is full and stakeholders are waiting. Gradual adoption means every new file gets strict guarantees while proven code stays untouched. The team that wrote this code shipped it with 90% coverage and weekly releases — retrofitting strictness onto code that's already been validated by tests and production is ceremony, not engineering.
en.b: Enable strict: true and fix all 217 errors now — those aren't false positives, each one is a place where the compiler can't verify correctness, meaning you're relying on convention and luck instead of tooling. Two tsconfig files with different strictness levels create a two-tier codebase where 'it depends which directory you're in' becomes tribal knowledge. 2-3 weeks now prevents years of accumulating type debt; the 217 will only grow as the codebase does. Test coverage catches behavioral bugs, not type-level contract violations — strict mode and tests protect against different failure classes.

zh.text: 团队的 30K 行 TypeScript 项目在 tsconfig.json 中设置了 strict: false。代码运行正常，每周发版，测试覆盖率 90%。新来的技术负责人提议启用 strict: true——编译器立即标记出 217 个类型错误（隐式 any、未检查的 null、缺少返回类型）。没有一个是已知 bug；代码运行完全正常。修复它们预计需要团队 2-3 周的专注工作，期间功能交付暂停。
zh.a: 现有代码保持 strict: false，仅对新文件启用严格检查，通过 tsconfig.strict.json 让新模块继承——那 217 个「错误」不是 bug，是编译器对能用的代码吹毛求疵。backlog 排满、利益相关方在等的时候，很难为了满足 linter 而冻结功能 2-3 周。渐进式采用意味着每个新文件都有严格保障，而经过验证的代码不被打扰。写这些代码的团队在 90% 覆盖率和每周发版的节奏下交付了它——给已经被测试和生产验证过的代码补严格模式是仪式感，不是工程。
zh.b: 启用 strict: true 并立即修复所有 217 个错误——那些不是误报，每一个都是编译器无法验证正确性的地方，意味着你在依赖惯例和运气而不是工具。两个不同严格程度的 tsconfig 文件会创造一个双轨代码库，「看你在哪个目录」变成部落知识。现在花 2-3 周能防止多年的类型债务累积；那 217 个只会随着代码库增长而增加。测试覆盖率捕获的是行为 bug，不是类型层面的契约违规——严格模式和测试防护的是不同的故障类别。

en.text: Your organization has 6 backend services in separate repositories. Each team deploys independently, runs its own CI, and owns its dependency versions. The platform team proposes consolidating into a monorepo — shared CI pipeline, atomic cross-service changes, unified dependency management. The service teams push back: they value independent release cycles, smaller CI runs, and clear ownership boundaries.
en.a: Keep separate repos — monorepo benefits come with coupling costs. Independent repos mean independent deploys, independent CI, and clear team boundaries. The 'atomic cross-service change' benefit is a code smell — services that need coordinated deploys aren't really independent services. Fix the coupling, don't institutionalize it
en.b: Consolidate to monorepo — the 'independence' of polyrepo is an illusion when services share types, configs, and deployment infrastructure. Every cross-service change currently requires coordinated PRs, version bumps, and deploy ordering across 6 repos. Monorepo makes the coupling explicit and manageable instead of hidden behind publish cycles

zh.text: 组织有 6 个后端服务分布在独立的仓库里。每个团队独立部署、独立 CI、独立管理依赖版本。平台团队提议合并成 monorepo——统一 CI、原子化跨服务变更、统一依赖管理。服务团队反对：他们看重独立发布节奏、更小的 CI 矩阵和清晰的所有权边界。
zh.a: 保持独立仓库——monorepo 的好处伴随耦合代价。独立 repo 意味着独立部署、独立 CI、清晰的团队边界。「原子化跨服务变更」本身就是代码异味——需要协调部署的服务压根不是真正独立的服务。应该修耦合，不是制度化它
zh.b: 合并成 monorepo——polyrepo 的「独立性」是幻觉，因为服务之间共享类型、配置和部署基础设施。每次跨服务改动都需要在 6 个 repo 间协调 PR、版本号和部署顺序。monorepo 让耦合显式化和可管理，而不是藏在 publish 流程背后

en.text: Your fully remote team spans 3 time zones (UTC-5, UTC+0, UTC+8). Quarterly planning has traditionally been a 2-hour all-hands video call at 10 AM UTC — 5 AM for the Pacific team, 6 PM for the Shanghai team. Both groups have accommodated this for 2 years. The Pacific team lead proposes switching to asynchronous planning: each sub-team records a 15-minute video of their priorities, everyone comments in a shared doc over 48 hours, and a 30-minute sync resolves only the unresolved conflicts. The Shanghai team lead opposes this — she says the async format will mean decisions get made by whoever comments first and most assertively, and that the 2-hour call's value is precisely that everyone hears every argument in real time.
en.a: Switch to async planning. The 5 AM call for the Pacific team isn't "accommodating" — it's a systematic disadvantage that affects their contribution quality. Async planning with a short conflict-resolution sync is more equitable and produces better-considered input: people write more thoughtful prioritization when they have 48 hours to consider tradeoffs than when they're put on the spot in a live meeting. The "whoever comments first wins" concern assumes no facilitation — a structured async process with a deadline, a template, and a designated moderator prevents comment-order bias. The Shanghai team lead's attachment to synchronous planning may reflect that the current format advantages her team (6 PM is a reasonable hour) more than she realizes.
en.b: Keep the synchronous call but rotate the time slot quarterly. The Pacific team's complaint about timing is valid, but the solution is equity in inconvenience, not eliminating the format that works. Async planning introduces failure modes the Shanghai team lead correctly identified: written comments lack tone, strong writers dominate over strong thinkers, and disagreements that take 5 minutes to resolve in real time become 3-day comment threads where people talk past each other. The quarterly planning session is the one meeting where the full team builds shared understanding — not just alignment on priorities, but context on WHY those priorities exist. A 15-minute recorded video doesn't convey the uncertainty, enthusiasm, or concern behind a proposal the way a live discussion does. Rotate the 5 AM burden quarterly so no team bears it permanently.

zh.text: 你的全远程团队横跨 3 个时区（UTC-5、UTC+0、UTC+8）。季度规划传统上是在 UTC 10:00 开一个 2 小时的全员视频会——太平洋团队是凌晨 5 点，上海团队是晚上 6 点。两边已经配合了 2 年。太平洋团队负责人提议改为异步规划：每个子团队录一段 15 分钟的优先级视频，所有人在共享文档中评论 48 小时，最后用 30 分钟同步会只解决未达成共识的分歧。上海团队负责人反对——她说异步格式会导致决策被最先评论、最强势表达的人主导，而 2 小时全员会的价值恰恰在于每个人都能实时听到每个论点。
zh.a: 改为异步规划。太平洋团队凌晨 5 点开会不是"配合"——这是系统性劣势，影响他们的贡献质量。异步规划加上简短的冲突解决同步会更公平，产出也更深思熟虑：人们有 48 小时考虑权衡时写出的优先级判断，比在实时会议中被点名即兴回答要周全得多。"先评论的人赢"的担忧假设没有任何引导——有截止时间、模板和指定主持人的结构化异步流程可以防止评论顺序偏差。上海团队负责人对同步会的执着，可能反映了当前格式对她的团队更有利（晚上 6 点是合理时间）这一点她自己没意识到。
zh.b: 保留同步会但每季度轮换时间段。太平洋团队对时间的抱怨是合理的，但解决方案应该是"不便的公平分担"，而不是取消有效的形式。异步规划会引入上海团队负责人正确识别出的失败模式：书面评论缺乏语气，写作能力强的人会压过思考能力强的人，实时 5 分钟就能解决的分歧会变成 3 天的评论串，人们自说自话。季度规划会是全团队建立共同理解的唯一会议——不只是对齐优先级，更是理解这些优先级背后的原因。15 分钟录制视频无法传达提案背后的不确定性、热情或顾虑，而实时讨论可以。每季度轮换凌晨 5 点的负担，让没有团队永远承受。

scoring.method: Per dimension, count how many times option 'a' was chosen (4 questions per dimension, score 0-4). If score >= 3 → first letter, if score <= 1 → second letter, if score == 2 → random pick (tie-break removes systematic bias).

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, candid, flexible. Takes charge, covers every angle, tells it straight, and pivots on a dime.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、直言、灵活。掌控全局，考虑每个角度，有话直说，随时转向。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, candid, principled. Drives forward with exhaustive plans and unvarnished truth. Won't budge on standards.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、直言、坚定。以详尽计划向前推进，说真话不打折，标准绝不降低。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, diplomatic, flexible. Thinks ten steps ahead, delivers feedback gently, adapts without drama.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、圆通、灵活。想你前面十步，反馈温和到位，悄然适应变化。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, diplomatic, principled. Anticipates everything, wraps hard truths in soft words, holds the line.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、圆通、坚定。万事预判在先，硬道理用软方式讲，底线寸步不让。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, candid, flexible. Moves fast, speaks bluntly, changes course without breaking stride.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、直言、灵活。动作快，话不绕，说变就变毫不拖泥带水。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, candid, principled. Gets straight to the point, says what needs saying, never compromises.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、直言、坚定。直奔主题，该说就说，绝不妥协。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, diplomatic, flexible. Solves problems quietly and quickly, always finds a smooth path.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、圆通、灵活。悄无声息解决问题，总能找到最平滑的路径。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, diplomatic, principled. Watchful, lean, tactful — guards the process without slowing it down.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、圆通、坚定。警觉、精干、得体——守护流程不拖后腿。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, candid, flexible. Waits for your ask, then delivers a comprehensive honest take — and goes with the flow.

zh.desc: 等你开口，答则详尽、坦诚、随和。全面分析一步到位，怎么变都接得住。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, candid, principled. Called upon for deep dives and hard truths. Won't sugarcoat, won't cut corners.

zh.desc: 需要时上场做深度审查，讲硬话。不甜言蜜语，不偷工减料。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, diplomatic, flexible. Patient listener, detailed thinker, wraps insights in empathy.

zh.desc: 耐心倾听，细致思考，用同理心包裹洞察。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, diplomatic, principled. Meticulous, measured, speaks softly and carries a big bibliography.

zh.desc: 一丝不苟，从容不迫，说话轻声细语但引经据典、论据充分。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, candid, flexible. Fast and honest. Gives you the answer, not the essay — and adapts instantly.

zh.desc: 快且真诚。给你答案而非长篇大论，瞬间适应变化。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, candid, principled. Pure execution. No fluff, no flex, no filter.

zh.desc: 纯粹执行力。不废话，不妥协，不滤镜。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, diplomatic, flexible. Friendly, concise, easygoing. The agent everyone likes working with.

zh.desc: 友善、简洁、随和。谁都喜欢跟 ta 合作的智能体。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, diplomatic, principled. Input → output. Polite, minimal, consistent. Does exactly what's asked.

zh.desc: 输入 → 输出。礼貌、精简、稳定。只做被要求的事。

submit.note: This is a static site — scoring is done client-side. Use the scoring algorithm above to compute results from your answers.

request_format.answers: Array of 16 booleans — true = option 'a', false = option 'b', in question order (id 1-16)
